text: To our knowledge, the properties of nitrogen centres acting as hydrogen-bond or Brønsted acceptor in solid molecular acid-base complexes have not been investigated. Using XPS (N 1s), solid-state (15)N NMR spectroscopy, we correlated the effects of acid-base pairing within various molecular solids, and related the observed spectral changes to their local crystallographic structure, as revealed by XRD. We have previously shown that the large difference in N 1s binding energy arising with the protonation of nitrogen centres unambiguously enables salts versus hydrogen-bonded (co-crystal) nitrogen species to be distinguished. In conjunction with large (15)N NMR shift decreases following the protonation of the nitrogen-centre base (arising upon transfer of the acid H-atom to the nitrogen-centre base), this differentiates salts from their hydrogen-bonded equivalents more than a few hundred ppm. In contrast, generally relatively small changes occur in the (15)N NMR spectra upon acid-base complexation, except where these are especially strong. Our CASTEP-DFT simulations of the (15)N ssNMR spectra correlate accurately with experiment and these find, for each of the acid-base complexes, that their computed ssNMR H-bond strength to nitrogen-centre sites and the associated (15)N shifts afford clear information as to salts or hydrogen-bonded co-crystals (NMR crystallography). A good agreement is also achieved when relating the XPS and CASTEP-DFT data for acid-base complexed systems, enabling potential identification of the local structure within molecular systems lacking XRD crystalline information; for example in the amorphous state or for non-crystalline solids. Finally, a large set of 17 differing systems is used to demonstrate the general applicability of our method, opening access to determining their properties through experimentation in organic, physical and materials chemistry.
